text.title: go-backend Failure Modes
text.summary: Five antipatterns each with symptom / root-cause / fix.

antipattern.description: Defining interfaces in the impl package (repository defines UserRepository; service imports repository to import the interface).
antipattern.symptom: Build fails with "import cycle not allowed"; or developers add `interface` files in repository/ to dodge cycles.
antipattern.root-cause: Misreading Go interface ownership — interfaces belong to the consumer, not the producer.
antipattern.fix: Move the interface declaration to service/. repository/ has only concrete types implementing it. service depends on nothing except its own interfaces.

antipattern.description: Capturing gin.Context (`c`) in a goroutine: `go func() { h.userService.Process(c) }()`.
antipattern.symptom: Sporadic data races flagged by go test -race; request-context leaks beyond request lifetime; trace IDs propagate to wrong goroutines.
antipattern.root-cause: gin.Context is request-scoped and not thread-safe.
antipattern.fix: Before spawning, derive `ctx := c.Request.Context()` and extract any other values (params, headers). Pass ctx and the extracted values to the goroutine.

antipattern.description: Calling c.JSON(...) directly for error responses, bypassing the ErrorHandler middleware.
antipattern.symptom: Inconsistent error shapes across endpoints; ErrorHandler logs miss the actual failures; metrics undercount errors.
antipattern.root-cause: Habit from net/http or framework-less code; ErrorHandler convention not enforced.
antipattern.fix: Handlers return after `c.Error(err)`; ErrorHandler middleware reads `c.Errors` and writes the canonical response. CI grep blocks `c.JSON(http.Status[45]`.

antipattern.description: Bloating cmd/api/main.go with database setup, service init, business logic — 200+ lines.
antipattern.symptom: main.go grows; tests cannot exercise startup logic; agents add more glue inside main.
antipattern.root-cause: No explicit "thin main" rule; agents put everything where it visibly runs.
antipattern.fix: Move config loading, DB connect, service init into internal/config + internal/server. main.go limited to call sequence: cfg := config.Load(); deps := server.Wire(cfg); server.Run(deps).

antipattern.description: Pool.Submit that blocks on a full buffered channel without selecting on ctx.Done().
antipattern.symptom: Pool deadlocks under cancellation; goroutines stuck on send; graceful shutdown hangs.
antipattern.fix: `select { case p.jobs <- job: case <-ctx.Done(): return ctx.Err() }`. Worker close happens after sender finishes.
